zh.Cornhill, Northumberland, England: 英國諾圖波藍德可恩爾
zh.lifeSummary: William de Cornhill於1160年出生在英國諾圖波藍德可恩爾。他和Juliana至少有1個女兒。他去世於1223年享年63歲。

zh-hans.Cornhill, Northumberland, England: 英国諾圖波藍德可恩爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: William de Cornhill于1160年出生在英国諾圖波藍德可恩爾。他和Juliana至少有1个女儿。他去世于1223年享年63岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cornhill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: locative name in SE England mainly from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumb) or one of the many minor places so called. It has often become   Cornell   and has sometimes been confused with   Cornwall  , and   Cornwell  , as in the case of Thomas Cornell, 1722 in   Parish Registers   (Horringer, Suffolk), who is also called Cornwall (1736), Cornwell (1740) and Cornhill (1766).
2  English:  see  Cornall  .
3  English:  see  Cornwall  .

variants.name: Cornwall
meanings: English:

habitational name from the county of Cornwall, which is named with the Old English tribal name Cornwealas. This is from Kernow (the term that the Cornish used to refer to themselves, a word of uncertain etymology, perhaps connected with a Celtic element meaning ‘horn, headland’), + Old English wealas ‘strangers, foreigners’, the term used by the Anglo-Saxons for British-speaking people.
variant of   Cornwell  .

variants.name: Parish Registers
meanings: Huguenot: relationship name, probably from the Old Danish personal name Dūsi + the suffix -ing, denoting ‘son of Dūsi’. There appears to have been some confusion with Dowson : John Dowsyng, 1584, John Dowsen, 1589 in Parish Registers (Ickworth, Suffolk).

variants.name: Cornwell
meanings: English:
habitational name from Cornwell in Oxfordshire, named from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

variant of Cornwall or Cornhill; in southeastern England mainly a habitational name from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumberland) or one of the many minor places so called, often becoming Cornell .

variants.name: Cornell
meanings: English: shortened form of Cornwell , Cornwall , or of Cornhill, a habitational name from a place in Northumberland named Cornhill, from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + halh ‘nook, recess’; or from Cornhill in London, a medieval grain exchange, named with Old English corn ‘corn, grain’ + hyll ‘hill’, or from some other place elsewhere similarly named.
English: variant of   Corney  .
English: possibly also a nickname from French corneille ‘rook, crow’, probably denoting a chatterer or someone with dark hair or a dark complexion.

variants.name: Cornall
meanings: 1 English: the Lancs name is a variant of Corney , where -all and -ell are hypercorrect pronunciations of Cornah, Corner, and Cornoe, late pronunciations of Corney.

2 English: the Devon name is a variant of Cornwall . Here also probably belongs Gurnall, as in Joan Gurnall, 1586 in IGI (Crediton, Devon), and William Gurnall, 1650 in IGI (Lavenham, Suffolk). Compare Corney .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

en.lifeSummary: William de Cornhill was born in 1160, in Cornhill, Northumberland, England. He had at least 1 daughter with Juliana. He died in 1223, at the age of 63.

fr.lifeSummary: William de Cornhill est né en 1160, à Cornhill, Northumberland, Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Juliana. Il est décédé en 1223, à l’âge de 63 ans.

de.lifeSummary: William de Cornhill wurde 1160 in Cornhill, Northumberland, England geboren. Er hatte mit Juliana mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1223 im Alter von 63 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: William de Cornhill è nato il nel 1160 nel Cornhill, Northumberland, England. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Juliana. Morì nel 1223, all'età di 63 anni.

ja.Cornhill, Northumberland, England: イギリスノースウンバーランドコルンヒル
ja.Northamptonshire, England: イギリスノーサンプトンシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: William de Cornhillは1160年にイギリスノースウンバーランドコルンヒルで生まれた。彼はJulianaとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1223年に63歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Cornhill(은)는 1160년 영국 노르스벌랜드 코른힐에서 태어났다. 그는 Juliana(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1223년 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: William de Cornhill nasceu em 1160, em Cornhill, Northumberland, Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Juliana. Ele faleceu em 1223, com 63 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: William de Cornhill родился в 1160 г. в местности корнилл, нортумбирланд, Англия. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Juliana. Он умер в 1223 г. в возрасте 63 лет.

es.lifeSummary: William de Cornhill nació en 1160, en Cornhill, Northumberland, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Juliana. Murió en 1223, a la edad de 63 años.
